slides.title: The free fix for *bloating after meals*.
slides.sub: Digestion starts in your mouth. Chewing thoroughly releases enzymes and gives your gut time to signal that you're full.

slides.title: Digestion starts in your mouth
slides.body: Chewing breaks food down and releases *salivary enzymes* that start digesting before food even reaches your stomach.

slides.title: Aim for 20–30 chews
slides.body: Most people swallow after a few. *20–30 chews per bite* gives enzymes and gut signals time to work.

slides.title: Smaller pieces, easier gut
slides.body: Big chunks ferment and produce gas. Chewing well can mean *less bloating and discomfort* after meals.

slides.title: Fullness needs 20 minutes
slides.body: Your gut takes time to signal fullness. Slowing down lets the signal land *before you overeat*.

slides.title: Put the fork down
slides.body: Set utensils down between bites. This one habit can *halve your eating speed* without any willpower.

slides.title: Eat slower, *feel lighter*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your meals and ties how you eat to how you feel — so comfort patterns get clear in your daily score.
slides.tag: better digestion, one bite at a time

caption:
Bloated after meals? The fix is free and you already own it. 🍴

Digestion starts in your mouth — chewing 20–30 times per bite releases enzymes, cuts gas, and gives your gut the 20 minutes it needs to feel full. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health ties how you eat to how you feel so the pattern gets obvious → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Free Fix For Bloating After Meals
